channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: US-Canada-Mexico joint World Cup goes from unity to acrimony thanks to tariffs and '51st state' talk
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Seven years ago, when a joint bid by the United States, Canada and Mexico was awarded the 2026 World Cup, rifts created by tariffs — yes, back then, too! — and a proposed border wall were glossed over because of the neighbors’ longstanding political and economic alliances.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Carson Kelly becomes 1st Cubs player to hit for the cycle in 32 years in 18-3 rout of A's in West Sacramento
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Carson Kelly had come close to hitting for the cycle, yet that elusive triple kept holding him back. He had just two triples over a 10-year big league career before Monday night. That's when Kelly became the first Cubs player in 32 years to hit for the cycle.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Favorite UConn joins 3 top seeds in South Carolina, UCLA and Texas in women’s Final Four
item.description: No room for Cinderellas in Tampa this week with No. 1 seeds South Carolina, UCLA and Texas in the national semifinals.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Paige Bueckers carries UConn to record 24th women's Final Four with 78-64 win over USC
item.description: SPOKANE, Wash. (AP) — Paige Bueckers and her UConn teammates didn’t cut down the net after securing a trip to the Final Four, even though a ladder was set up and ready to go. The reason was simple: The Huskies aren’t done yet.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Betting favorite UConn joins 3 top seeds in South Carolina, UCLA and Texas in women's Final Four
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — The women's Final Four is full of star players, top seeds and historic programs. There will be no Cinderellas in Tampa, Florida, this week with No. 1 seeds South Carolina, UCLA and Texas in the national semifinals.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Madison Booker helps Texas reach its first women's Final Four since 2003 with 58-47 win over TCU
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — Rori Harmon put her hands on her knees and bowed her head as the clock wound down. The Texas senior guard doesn't like crying, but she gave herself a moment to reflect on the past 10 months. This time last year, Harmon was sidelined by a knee injury.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Future of the "tush push" comes down to a vote by NFL owners
item.description: The future of the “tush push” should be decided on Tuesday following debates about the safety of a play that’s helped the Philadelphia Eagles win one Super Bowl and reach another.
item.dc:creator: Rob Maaddi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jazz clinch worst record in franchise history
item.description: CHARLOTTE (ABC4 Sports) - It's official. The 2024-25 Utah Jazz will have the worst record in franchise history. Miles Bridges had 26 points, 10 rebounds and eight assists and the Charlotte Hornets defeated the Jazz 110-106 on Monday night hand Utah is 60th loss of the season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Federal agency gives Maine final warning to ban trans athletes from girls' sports
item.description: The Department of Education has given Maine a final warning to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order to ban trans athletes from girls' sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shower slip knocks Dodgers 1B Freddie Freeman out of lineup after hurting surgically repaired ankle
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Los Angeles Dodgers first baseman Freddie Freeman was out of the lineup Monday night against the Atlanta Braves after slipping in the shower and hurting his surgically repaired right ankle. “He had a little mishap entering the shower,” manager Dave Roberts said.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Milwaukee pays tribute to Bob Uecker in Brewers' first home game since his death
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — As the Milwaukee Brewers conducted a moment of silence before their home opener to honor Bob Uecker, one fan decided keeping quiet wasn’t the best way to pay tribute to the lovable loquacious broadcaster. “We love you, Ueck,” the spectator shouted.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maine school officials explain why state was in 'noncompliance with Title IX' after referral to DOJ
item.description: Maine school officials explained why they were unable to sign the Trump administration's agreement to keep trans athletes out of girls' and women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lee Cummard named BYU women's basketball coach
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU has named Lee Cummard as its next women's basketball head coach. Cummard has been the interim head coach since Amber Whiting resigned after three seasons on March 8.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Defending champion South Carolina returns to Final Four of women's NCAA Tournament
item.description: Chloe Kitts scored 14 points and defending national champion South Carolina reached the Final Four of the women's NCAA Tournament for a fifth straight year, beating Duke 54-50 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Russell Wilson is heading to the New York Giants, AP source says
item.description: Russell Wilson has agreed on a one-year contract with the New York Giants, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, NewsNation
